assertions.title: Reset wallet clears session, secret, and vault; returns to Welcome; next biometric is fresh
assertions.reason: The focused cross-area suite passed its reset flow, the supporting agent-store reset suite passed, and the saved implementation scan shows reset delegating to vault/native secret deletion, persistent flag removal, LevelDB wipe, teardown, and session reset before the post-reset route matrix returns to Welcome; the second first-launch stores fresh native secret material and yields a different DID.

commands: rg -n -e 'VAL-CROSS-006' -e 'reset clears all state \+ a second first-launch produces a different DID' -e 'deleteSecret' -e 'destroyAgentLevelDatabases' -e 'expect\(secondDid\)\.not\.toBe\(firstDid\)' src/__tests__/cross-area/reset-and-restore-flow.test.tsx

rg -n -e 'NEVER writes the mnemonic to SecureStorage during first-launch' -e 'calls NativeBiometricVault.deleteSecret, tears down the agent, and resets the session store' -e 'expect\(nativeBiometric\.deleteSecret\.mock\.calls\.length\)\.toBeGreaterThanOrEqual\(1\)' -e 'expect\(sessionState\.isLocked\)\.toBe\(true\)' src/lib/enbox/__tests__/agent-store.recoveryPhrase.test.ts

rg -n -e 'reset: async \(\)' -e 'await vault\.reset\(\)' -e 'NativeBiometricVault\.deleteSecret' -e 'destroyAgentLevelDatabases' -e 'get\(\)\.teardown\(\)' -e 'useSessionStore\.getState\(\)\.reset\(\)' -e 'remove\(INITIALIZED_STORAGE_KEY\)' -e 'remove\(BIOMETRIC_STATE_STORAGE_KEY\)' src/lib/enbox/agent-store.ts src/lib/enbox/biometric-vault.ts src/features/session/session-store.ts
testNames: reset clears all state + a second first-launch produces a different DID
calls NativeBiometricVault.deleteSecret, tears down the agent, and resets the session store

assertions.title: Recovery-phrase restore determinism
assertions.reason: The reset-and-restore cross-area suite passed the same-mnemonic and different-mnemonic branches, proving restore determinism across resets, and the supporting recovery-phrase store suite passed its guard that the first-launch mnemonic is never written to SecureStorage.

commands: rg -n -e 'VAL-CROSS-007' -e 'restore with the original mnemonic yields the original DID; a different mnemonic yields a different DID' -e 'expect\(didRestored\)\.toBe\(did1\)' -e 'expect\(didAlt\)\.not\.toBe\(did1\)' src/__tests__/cross-area/reset-and-restore-flow.test.tsx
rg -n -e 'VAL-VAULT-018' -e 'NEVER writes the mnemonic to SecureStorage during first-launch' src/lib/enbox/__tests__/agent-store.recoveryPhrase.test.ts
testNames: restore with the original mnemonic yields the original DID; a different mnemonic yields a different DID
NEVER writes the mnemonic to SecureStorage during first-launch

assertions.title: Biometric invalidation surfaces recovery-restore path
assertions.reason: The focused cross-area suite passed the invalidation flow end-to-end on the Jest surface: the unlock path rejects with the JS wrapper's documented equivalent code `VAULT_ERROR_KEY_INVALIDATED`, the session is marked invalidated, the route matrix selects RecoveryRestore, and a valid mnemonic restore returns the session to ready/unlocked state; the supporting RecoveryRestoreScreen suite also passed the invalid-mnemonic branch, keeping the screen in its locked invalidated state with an inline alert and no restore call.

commands: rg -n -e 'VAL-CROSS-009' -e 'KEY_INVALIDATED on unlock flips biometricStatus and valid mnemonic restore rewires to ready' -e "setBiometricStatus\('invalidated'\)" -e "toBe\('RecoveryRestore'\)" -e "getByLabelText\('Restore wallet'\)" -e "biometricStatus\)\.toBe\('ready'\)" src/__tests__/cross-area/reset-and-restore-flow.test.tsx

rg -n -e 'isLocked: true' -e 'shows a clear role="alert" message on an invalid mnemonic and does NOT call restore' -e 'rejects a short mnemonic with an inline alert and never calls restore' -e 'biometricStatus\)\.toBe\(\'invalidated\'\)' src/features/auth/screens/recovery-restore-screen.test.tsx
testNames: KEY_INVALIDATED on unlock flips biometricStatus and valid mnemonic restore rewires to ready
shows a clear role="alert" message on an invalid mnemonic and does NOT call restore
rejects a short mnemonic with an inline alert and never calls restore

commandsRun.observation: PASS; the focused cross-area suite passed all 3 assigned reset/restore/invalidation tests.

commandsRun.command: rg -n -e 'VAL-CROSS-006' -e 'reset clears all state \+ a second first-launch produces a different DID' -e 'deleteSecret' -e 'destroyAgentLevelDatabases' -e 'expect\(secondDid\)\.not\.toBe\(firstDid\)' -e 'VAL-CROSS-007' -e 'restore with the original mnemonic yields the original DID; a different mnemonic yields a different DID' -e 'expect\(didRestored\)\.toBe\(did1\)' -e 'expect\(didAlt\)\.not\.toBe\(did1\)' -e 'VAL-CROSS-009' -e 'KEY_INVALIDATED on unlock flips biometricStatus and valid mnemonic restore rewires to ready' -e "setBiometricStatus\('invalidated'\)" -e "toBe\('RecoveryRestore'\)" -e "getByLabelText\('Restore wallet'\)" -e "biometricStatus\)\.toBe\('ready'\)" src/__tests__/cross-area/reset-and-restore-flow.test.tsx

PASS; the RecoveryRestoreScreen suite passed all 17 tests, including the invalid-mnemonic and successful-restore behaviors that support the cross-area invalidation flow.

commandsRun.command: rg -n -e 'isLocked: true' -e 'shows a clear role="alert" message on an invalid mnemonic and does NOT call restore' -e 'rejects a short mnemonic with an inline alert and never calls restore' -e 'biometricStatus\)\.toBe\(\'invalidated\'\)' -e 'mockRestoreFromMnemonic\)\.not\.toHaveBeenCalled\(\)' src/features/auth/screens/recovery-restore-screen.test.tsx
commandsRun.observation: PASS; the saved line scan captured the invalidated locked setup plus the invalid-mnemonic alert/no-restore expectations used to support VAL-CROSS-009 step 7.
PASS; the support suite passed all 8 tests, including the no-mnemonic-persistence guard and reset orchestration assertions.

commandsRun.observation: PASS; the saved scan located the supporting no-mnemonic-persistence and reset state assertions.

commandsRun.command: rg -n -e 'reset: async \(\)' -e 'await vault\.reset\(\)' -e 'NativeBiometricVault\.deleteSecret' -e 'destroyAgentLevelDatabases' -e 'get\(\)\.teardown\(\)' -e 'useSessionStore\.getState\(\)\.reset\(\)' -e 'remove\(INITIALIZED_STORAGE_KEY\)' -e 'remove\(BIOMETRIC_STATE_STORAGE_KEY\)' -e "KEY_INVALIDATED_CODE = 'VAULT_ERROR_KEY_INVALIDATED'" -e "_biometricState = 'invalidated'" -e 'hydrateRestored\(\)' -e 'restoreFromMnemonic\(normalized\)' src/lib/enbox/agent-store.ts src/lib/enbox/biometric-vault.ts src/features/session/session-store.ts src/features/auth/screens/recovery-restore-screen.tsx

summary: Tested 3 assigned cross reset/restore assertions on the jest-local surface: 3 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. The focused reset-and-restore suite passed all assigned tests, and supporting RecoveryRestoreScreen and agent-store suites supplied the missing invalid-mnemonic and no-mnemonic-persistence evidence cited by the flow report.
